title: The Life That Now Is

sections.label: Verse 1
lines: The Spirit spoke, and spoke it low:
in the latter times, some let go,
giving heed to whispering things,
to doctrines dressed in angel wings.
They tell their lies with steady eyes,
a conscience seared, a slow disguise.

sections.label: Verse 2
lines: They'll forbid the wedding vow,
name the gift a burden now,
call unclean what God called good —
but every creature, understood,
is holy bread when hands are raised,
made clean by the Word and praise.

sections.label: Pre-Chorus
lines: So refuse the fable, keep the flame,
train the flesh — it fades the same;
the body profits little here...

sections.label: Chorus
lines: But godliness is gain in every hour,
a slow, unfailing, quiet power —
o hold the promise, hold it fast:
the life that now is, and the life to come,
the life that now is, and the life to come.

sections.label: Verse 3
lines: Let no man scorn your tender years;
be a lamp against the fears —
in word, in love, in faith made pure,
a good example, tried and sure.
Give your hours to holy reading,
to the doctrine, to the pleading.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: And godliness is gain in every hour,
a slow, unfailing, quiet power —
o hold the promise, hold it fast:
the life that now is, and the life to come,
the life that now is, and the life to come.

sections.label: Bridge
lines: We labor on, we bear the scorn,
we trust the God who holds the morn —
the living God, the one who saves,
who calls His many from their graves.
hold on, hold on

sections.label: Final Chorus
lines: So godliness is gain in every hour,
a slow, unfailing, rising power —
o hold the promise, hold it fast:
the life that now is, and the life to come.

sections.label: Outro
lines: Take heed, take heed, and don't let go;
neglect not the gift you were given to grow.
Continue on — and this is true:
you'll save yourself, and those who hear you.
The life that now is... and the life to come.

epigraph: that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith
